title: [Bug]: Labels metadata header accepts ASCII control characters
summary: The labels metadata header validator rejects newlines but still accepts other ASCII control characters.
observed: A configuration can set message_metadata.labels.header to a control-character-bearing value and validation accepts it.
expected: Labels metadata header names should use the same fail-closed control-character policy as mission metadata, security labels, and WebSocket headers.
reproduction: Construct MessageMetadataLabelsConfig with a header containing an ASCII control character and observe that validation succeeds.
failing_test: Add a unit test that creates MessageMetadataLabelsConfig(header='Labels\u0000Header') and expects validation to fail.
impact: Ambiguous header names can interfere with safe metadata extraction and configuration review.
delivery_semantics: Commit-then-ACK behavior is not changed. The defect is rejected during configuration loading.
security: The fix must reject hostile control characters without exposing configured values in logs.
acceptance: Labels metadata header validation rejects all ASCII control characters.
The regression test fails before the fix and passes after the fix.
The error explains that metadata headers must not contain control characters.
No sink storage schema or ACK behavior changes.
tests: Run the focused metadata boundary tests and the full local validation script.
documentation: Update release notes and test evidence.
closeout: Mark completed after merge with sanitized evidence.
